summary: Heavily-obfuscated bulk email/SMS abuse toolkit with browser-injection dropper
details: nolimit-x is a self-described 'red-team' email/SMS abuse toolkit shipped as 45+ heavily-obfuscated files under.ad/ (obfuscator.io string-array + RC4 layer). The CLI provides SMS-bombing via SMTP-to-carrier gateways (verizon/tmobile/att/cricket in.ad/x0.js), Twilio/Telnyx/Plivo SMS abuse (.ad/sms-providers.js references api.twilio.com, api.telnyx.com, api.plivo.com), bulk email sending, open-redirect scanning, Microsoft Graph / Gmail / IMAP contact-list scraping, and DKIM/SPF generation..ad/web-command.js drops a Chrome MV3 extension into the user's profile that opens a WebSocket to 127.0.0.1:9227, receives INJECT messages with arbitrary JS, and runs chrome.scripting.executeScript against Outlook/Gmail tabs — a persistent, hostile-by-design webmail-injection backdoor..ad/x4.js phones home to an obfuscated license server with HWID, hostname, and platform on `activate`/`serverVerify`. The harmful subcommands require user invocation rather than firing at npm install, but the package's only purpose is conducting spam/phishing/abuse campaigns and persistently subverting the operator's browser, and the obfuscation is intended to evade analysis. There is no legitimate developer use case.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
